title: deprea lutea
intro: Deprea lutea, also known as the yellow jimsonweed or yellow buffalo bell, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include acting as an antispasmodic, antitussive, and expectorant, helping to relieve muscle spasms, coughs, and congestion. The health benefits of Deprea lutea include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and alleviate anxiety. Deprea lutea is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
